CHECKLIST / MODULE 8 / LESSON 2: WEBINAR METRICS
WEBINAR METRICS CHECKLIST
WHAT TO TRACK
Overall Success
✓ 	Registration rate (% of visitors to the webinar registration page who
registered to attend)
✓ 	Attendance rate (% of registrants who actually attended)
✓ 	Purchases (# of people who bought)
Purchase Data
✓ 	% of registrants who purchased
✓ 	% of attendees who purchased
✓ 	Value per registrant (total sales $ / # of registrants)
✓ 	Value per attendee (total sales $ / # of registrants)
Attendance Statistics
✓ 	Average watch time
✓ 	# and % of attendees at the start of the offer
✓ 	# and % of attendees who saw the offer
✓ 	# and % of attendees who stayed until the end
Time on Webinar
✓ 	Drop off points (times when people stopped viewing)
Email
✓ 	Open rates for each email
✓ 	Clickthrough rates for each email
Replay Data
✓ 	Views
✓ 	Time viewed
✓ 	Drop off points
✓ 	Buy rates and ratios

CHECKLIST / MODULE 8 / LESSON 2: WEBINAR METRICS
Replay
✓ 	% of replay views who saw the sales page
✓ 	% of replay viewers who purchased
✓ 	% of non-registrants who saw replay
✓ 	% of non-registrants who purchased from replay
✓ 	% of non-attendees who saw replay
✓ 	% of non-attendees who purchased from replay
Cart Open Period
✓ 	Purchases by day during the Cart Open period
✓ 	Actions that created those purchases (emails, Q&A webinar, additional
bonuses, etc.)
✓ 	Sales on Cart Close day
Sales Page
✓ 	Long-form versus Abbreviated Sales Page results
✓ 	Sales video stats (% viewed, drop-off points, % who didn’t see the offer, %
who saw the offer)
Non-webinar registration ratios
✓ 	% of non-registrants who saw the sales page
✓ 	% of non-registrants who purchased
✓ 	%of non-attendees who saw sales page
✓ 	% of non-attendees who purchased
Ad Spend
✓ 	Total ad spending (by ad source, campaign, ad copy, targeting)
✓ 	Return on Ad Spend (ROAS = total sales / total ad spend). ROAS by:
✓ 	Ad source
✓ 	Campaign
✓ 	Strategy
✓ 	Ad copy
✓ 	Targeting

CHECKLIST / MODULE 8 / LESSON 2: WEBINAR METRICS
WHERE TO GET THE DATA
✓ 	Google Analytics, Wicket Reports, etc.
✓ 	Your webinar supplier’s analytics reporting
✓ 	Your email system (clicks, opens, attribution)
✓ 	Your shopping cart (purchases, attribution)
✓ 	Your video display tool (video time viewed data)
✓ 	Your ad supplier analytics (ad costs, clickthroughs, sales)
SETTING UP YOUR TRACKING AND ANALYTICS
✓ 	Put analytics on every page, even if it’s on a tech provider’s site (webinar
provider, shopping cart provider, etc.)
✓ 	Make sure that “cross-domain tracking” is set up properly in Google
Analytics
✓ 	Set up goals in Google Analytics to identify when actions have taken place
✓ 	Set tags in your email system to track when certain actions are taken
✓ 	Include your ad company’s pixels on every page
✓ 	Set up conversion tracking on sales pages and thank you pages for the
webinar
✓ 	Select a video hosting company that has analytics (so you can track drop-
off points on video views)
HOW TO USE & MONITOR THE DATA
✓ 	Look for underperforming statistics
✓ 	Make sure the data is accurate
✓ 	Gather as much data as you can
✓ 	Confirm the data (similar data from multiple sources)
✓ 	Debrief each webinar looking for what went well and what could be
improved
✓ 	Ideally you want to measure everything; in the beginning, set up a base
set of metrics then expand from there
